title: tournefortia incana
intro: Tournefortia incana, also known as the "Wild Olive Tree" or "Tournefortia", is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. It is a medicinal plant that has been used in traditional folk medicine for centuries, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, cold, and skin infections, and is also believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Tournefortia incana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and creams, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
